business_problem: Pinterest needed to scale AI-powered visual discovery to 600 million monthly active users while keeping the platform a positive, trusted, and safe experience differentiated from competitors who optimize for viral engagement.
solution_summary: Pinterest runs its recommendation system entirely on AWS, using Amazon EKS for rapid AI deployment, 10,000+ Amazon EC2 G5 instances for inference, 600+ P4/P4de instances for training, Amazon SageMaker for visual language models, and Amazon Bedrock for generative AI. It analyzes over 18 TB of data daily, processes more than 10 million AI-powered recommendations per second, and uses 500+ PB of storage on AWS. New features include Pinterest Canvas (a latent diffusion vision foundation model for image generation), visual search updates that recognize 2.5 billion objects, and Pinterest Assistant, a voice-enabled generative AI conversational experience.
business_outcome: Pinterest scaled AI-powered discovery to 600 million monthly active users while delivering revenue growth and user growth, driving 70% of user discovery through AI and significantly improving search fulfillment.
metrics: 600 million monthly active users
500+ petabytes of data scaled on AWS
10,000+ Amazon EC2 G5 instances for inference
600+ P4/P4de instances for training
18 terabytes of data analyzed daily
10 million AI-powered recommendations per second
2.5 billion objects recognized via visual search
17% year-over-year revenue growth
11% increase in monthly active users
230 basis point improvement in search fulfillment
70% of user discovery now AI-driven
architecture_clues: Microservices-based architecture on AWS since 2010
Recommendation system built entirely on AWS
Amazon EKS for rapid AI deployment
Pinterest Canvas: homegrown latent diffusion vision foundation model on EC2 GPU instances
Visual language models built using Amazon SageMaker
Pinterest Assistant: generative AI voice-enabled conversational experience
evidence_quotes: Built entirely on AWS, Pinterest uses Amazon EKS for rapid AI deployment across the business, 10,000+ Amazon EC2 G5 instances for inference and 600+ P4/P4de instances for training, analyzing over 18 terabytes of data daily.
Pinterest processes over 10 million AI-powered recommendations per second to support personalized discovery, advanced content moderation, and responsible features.
With the help of AWS, Pinterest scales processing, storage, and analysis of over 500 petabytes of data with ease.
17% year-over-year revenue growth ... 11% increase in monthly active users ... 230 basis point improvement in search fulfillment ... 70% of user discovery now AI-driven.

business_problem: Deutsche Börse Group's exchange, clearing and post-trade infrastructure — including Clearstream's central securities depository processing over one million transactions per day across more than 60 markets, SAP S/4HANA and the DAX index calculation — ran on stable but rigid on-premises systems where provisioning new environments took weeks or months, hardware bounded release cycles, and the platform was no longer keeping pace with evolving regulation and client demands for faster innovation.
solution_summary: Deutsche Börse Group re-engineered Clearstream's mission-critical workloads onto Google Cloud Compute Engine using N-, C4- and C4D-series machines plus Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme for latency-sensitive databases, built a multi-zone highly resilient architecture for zero data loss, migrated dozens of core financial apps including SAP S/4HANA, moved the DAX index calculation into the cloud, and runs OpenShift clusters on Compute Engine for portability — combined with Cortex, BigQuery and AI to accelerate downstream innovation.
business_outcome: Deutsche Börse Group lowered total cost of ownership, sharply improved settlement and DAX-index latency, cut SAP disaster-recovery time, and turned infrastructure provisioning from a months-long bottleneck into an on-demand capability that no longer limits delivery of new financial services.
metrics: 33% lower total cost of ownership
Settlement times improved up to 50%
Thousands of CPU cores and 60+ apps migrated
85% reduction in disaster recovery time for the critical SAP S/4HANA environment
50% less latency for the DAX index engine handling 13,000+ indices and ~30M ticks daily
More than 75% of workloads running in the cloud
Provisioning time reduced from months to hours
Development cycles for new financial services shortened from months to days
architecture_clues: Clearstream core financial apps run on Compute Engine N-series and latest C-series; latency-sensitive databases on C4D; broader app services on C4/N4
Hyperdisk Balanced and Hyperdisk Extreme underpin core database workloads
Multi-zone highly resilient architecture engineered for zero data loss while meeting regulatory requirements
OpenShift clusters on Compute Engine using Intel-based CPUs to preserve portability while standardizing on Google Cloud
SAP S/4HANA migrated from on-premises to a secure Google Cloud foundation
DAX family and EUROSTOXX50 index calculations executed in the cloud — 13,000+ indices, ~30M ticks/day
Cortex, BigQuery and AI used downstream to accelerate innovation and reduce cost
Customer-managed encryption keys, zone separation and regional failover safeguards as standard compliance controls
Dual strategy in progress: migrate traditional enterprise systems to Compute Engine, build new microservices on Compute Engine and GKE
evidence_quotes: 33% lower TCO
Settlement times improved up to 50% for greater efficiency
85% reduction in disaster recovery time for critical SAP S/4HANA systems
50% less latency for DAX® index engine: 13k+ indices, 30M ticks
Latency-sensitive databases run on high-performance C4D machines optimized for compute-intensive transaction processing, while C4/N4 instances support broader application services.
Clearstream engineered a multi-zone, highly resilient architecture on Google Cloud to achieve zero data loss for workloads while meeting strict regulatory requirements.

business_problem: FairPrice Group needed to keep pace with rapidly shifting retail consumer needs across more than 570 physical and digital touchpoints, personalize the in-store and online shopping journey, and equip more than its existing workforce with AI tools to streamline marketing, HR, customer service and store operations.
solution_summary: Through the 'Store of Tomorrow' program, FairPrice Group worked with Google Cloud to deploy a suite of agentic AI assistants in a newly opened AI-powered supermarket — cloud-connected shopping carts with in-cart conversational agents, a knowledge agent for recipe and pairing guidance, an inventory and product catalog search agent, a digital wine sommelier triggered by NFC, and a wellness assistant integrated with a Tanita body composition analyzer. Internally, employees use Gemini Enterprise inside Google Workspace and build custom agents (including a creative agent that generates promotional ads) using the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) and open standards such as Model Context Protocol.
business_outcome: Customers receive personalized, conversational shopping assistance across in-cart, search, wine and wellness scenarios while employees produce promotional content dramatically faster and build custom workflow agents inside Google Workspace.
metrics: Up to 18% click rate on in-cart assistant product recommendations
Ad creation time reduced by 10x
Ad creation cost reduced by up to 100x
architecture_clues: Cloud-connected shopping carts with integrated tablets running an in-cart conversational agent
In-cart speech agent powered by Chirp 2 speech recognition and the multimodal Gemini API
Knowledge agent built on Gemini API, Vertex AI RAG Engine, and the Grounding with Google Search API, triggered by scanning a product barcode
Product catalog and inventory search agent built on Vertex AI Search supporting local-context queries
Custom creative agent generating thematic ads using Imagen 4 and Veo 3 on Vertex AI plus Gemini API for ad copy
Digital wine sommelier using a multi-agent architecture triggered via NFC tap on the product
Wellness assistant in the Unity pharmacy: data agent calls the Tanita body composition API plus the Gemini API; conversational and search agents drive the workflow
Agents built using the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) with open standards including Model Context Protocol
Gemini Enterprise integrated with employees' Google Workspace, exposing Google-built agents (Deep Research, Idea Generation), FairPrice-built agents, and a no-code Agent Designer
evidence_quotes: Lets customers converse with in-cart assistants for personalized product recommendations and achieving up to 18% click rate
Reduces ad creation time by 10x while eliminating cost by up to 100x with Google Cloud
The 'Store of Tomorrow' program launched in Q3, 2025, when FairPrice Group opened the doors to its first AI-powered supermarket.
The conversational agent is driven by Chirp 2, the most advanced speech recognition model of Google Cloud, and the multimodal Gemini API.
Using a knowledge agent built on Gemini API, Vertex AI RAG Engine, and the Grounding with Google Search API, customers can get this information simply by scanning on the product's barcode.
Using Imagen 4 and Veo 3 models on Vertex AI, and the Gemini API to turn promotional details into ad copy, employees can create ads quickly and seamlessly

business_problem: Hershey Entertainment & Resorts (HE&R) needed to personalize experiences for highly varied guests across an amusement park, hotel, hockey team and entertainment venues, but its prior reliance on manual data sampling from Google Analytics and call recordings meant it could only review small slices of customer signals. The contact center also absorbed high volumes of repetitive calls — particularly seasonal-employee shift call-offs and password resets — diverting agents from complex guest issues.
solution_summary: HE&R centralized customer interaction and behavioral data in BigQuery, then used Vertex AI and Gemini to summarize social sentiment, employee engagement surveys and call data. It launched a natural-language website chatbot built on Conversational Agents that grew to handle invoice lookups via webhooks, and then built 'Agnes', an agentic IVR on Customer Engagement Suite that identifies callers via caller-ID webhooks, reads context such as account lockouts, and resolves common requests proactively.
business_outcome: HE&R automated a large share of repetitive guest and employee contact-center interactions, freed staff to focus on complex requests and innovation, and shortened the loop from raw interaction data to actionable reporting.
metrics: Natural-language customer chatbot implemented in less than 40 hours
More than 25% of team-member shift call-off requests fully automated
More than 50% of password reset calls managed by Customer Engagement Suite
A full eight hours of employee work saved per day on call-off handling
Dialogflow-to-BigQuery-to-Looker Studio reporting built in 30 minutes
architecture_clues: Unified customer interaction data stored in BigQuery as the single analytical store
Vertex AI and Gemini summarize social-channel sentiment (e.g. the 'Connoisseurs' Facebook page) and the annual employee engagement survey
Website chatbot powered by Conversational Agents extended with a webhook that takes an email address, checks for stays in the last 60 days, and emails a PDF invoice
Dialogflow interactions tagged with three answer fields are pushed to BigQuery and visualized in Looker Studio
'Agnes' IVR on Customer Engagement Suite uses caller-ID webhooks to identify callers and read context (account lockouts, failed logins, open support tickets) from HE&R's enterprise systems
Agnes proactively suggests password reset emails to locked-out users rather than presenting an IVR menu
evidence_quotes: Implemented a natural language customer chatbot in less than 40 hours
Fully automated more than 25% of team member 'call off' requests
Manages 50%+ of password reset calls with Customer Engagement Suite
That's a full eight hours of employee work per day. By saving those hours, our people can spend time innovating and solving more complex guest problems.
We can take our Dialogflow interactions, push them to BigQuery by answering three questions in Dialogflow, and then go to Looker Studio to create a report in 30 minutes.
Since Agnes is plugged into our infrastructure, it can see things like account lockouts, failed logins, or open support tickets

business_problem: Across more than 200 global brands, Kraft Heinz marketing and R&D teams had to keep pace with rapidly shifting consumer culture and personalize content, but new product concept and marketing content development was manual, took roughly eight weeks per cycle, and could not scale to the volume of personalized, on-brand assets the business wanted.
solution_summary: Working with Google Cloud Partner Apply Digital, Kraft Heinz built TasteMaker — a secure, prompt-less, web-based generative AI platform on Google Cloud that combines proprietary brand intelligence (brand guidelines, product catalog, photo libraries) consolidated in BigQuery with Vertex AI models including Gemini, Imagen and Veo to ideate product concepts, generate on-brand marketing imagery and video, evaluate content against audience signals, and power consumer-research testing.
business_outcome: TasteMaker became Kraft Heinz's unified AI platform for marketing and innovation, dramatically compressing concept-to-content time, cutting cost per asset, and giving non-technical marketers a prompt-less way to produce on-brand, personalized content at scale.
metrics: 8 hours instead of 8 weeks for new product content development
75% faster time to value for AI-supported processes
Thousands of hours saved by R&D, Innovation and Marketing teams
70% adoption rate among product development and marketing users on the platform
architecture_clues: Brand intelligence (brand guidelines, product catalog, photo libraries, consumer insights, culinary data) consolidated in BigQuery
Vertex AI orchestrates Gemini, Imagen and Veo for multimodal content generation
Gemini grounded in proprietary data evaluates and optimizes content for target audiences
Prompt-less, workflow-driven app suite designed for non-technical marketers
Built in partnership with Google Cloud Partner Apply Digital on the existing Google Cloud environment
evidence_quotes: 8 hours instead of 8 weeks for new product content development
Today the team sees a 70% rate of adoption among product development and marketing users who are on the TasteMaker platform.
Vertex AI integrates these models with Kraft Heinz's brand intelligence, a smorgasbord of proprietary data consolidated in BigQuery.
TasteMaker is a secure, prompt-less, web-based AI platform that runs on Google Cloud
Today, TasteMaker is our AI platform for all of marketing and innovation at Kraft Heinz

business_problem: Travel buyers were stuck with a 30-year-old 'tyranny of the five boxes' search paradigm while content sat scattered across many sources, and travel sellers (e.g. airlines) were absorbing tens of millions of customer emails a year for routine booking changes — both pressing Sabre to deliver more personalized travel commerce and free up customer-care agents for higher-value work.
solution_summary: After a five-year IT modernization that moved 99% of compute to Google Cloud, Sabre — working with partner Egen — built SabreMosaic, an AI-powered travel platform anchored by the Sabre IQ intelligence layer. Modules such as Ancillary IQ and Upgrade IQ use Gemini models, Vertex AI Agent Builder and semantic search on Vertex AI to drive personalized offers; SabreMosaic Concierge IQ, first adopted by Virgin Australia, is an agentic chat assistant built on Vertex AI Agent Builder, the latest Gemini models, Google Maps Platform and SabreMosaic APIs that handles natural-language trip planning, booking and management in one conversation.
business_outcome: Sabre is monetizing personalization for airlines, accelerating customer-care productivity, and shipping new agentic experiences on a modular API-based platform while running on a far cheaper and more resilient cloud foundation.
metrics: 70 ongoing generative AI projects
Up to 10% higher airline ancillary revenue via SabreMosaic Ancillary IQ
Up to 20% higher seat upgrade revenue via SabreMosaic Upgrade IQ
High single-digit productivity gains in customer-care operations from generative AI solutions
Up to 7% better market forecasting via Sabre Market Intelligence
$150M in annualized cost savings from cloud infrastructure modernization
99% of compute capacity now cloud-based
19 data centers decommissioned
More than 40,000 servers moved to the cloud
architecture_clues: Sabre IQ intelligence layer combining strong privacy controls, security and a governance framework on top of Google Cloud AI
SabreMosaic is a modular, open, API-based platform with selectable modules (Ancillary IQ, Upgrade IQ, Concierge IQ, Sabre Market Intelligence)
Concierge IQ agentic chat built on Vertex AI Agent Builder, latest Gemini models, Google Maps Platform, and SabreMosaic APIs
Semantic search on Vertex AI plus Gemini models underpin generative travel experiences
Real-time signal-based market forecasting via Sabre Market Intelligence rather than purely historical analysis
Sustainability data and flight carbon footprints exposed via the Google Travel Impact Model API
Automated AI agent testing and validation pipeline built with partner Egen, integrated into Sabre's existing dev workflow
evidence_quotes: 70 ongoing generative AI projects to improve travel experiences
Using AI to boost airline ancillary revenue up to 10% and seat upgrade revenue up to 20%
$150M in annualized cost savings through cloud infrastructure modernization
99% of compute capacity now cloud-based, unlocking AI-powered innovations

Sabre's latest development in this space is SabreMosaic™ Concierge IQ, an all-in-one generative AI chat solution for travelers built using Vertex AI Agent Builder, the latest Gemini models, Google Maps Platform, and SabreMosaic APIs.

business_problem: Shopify powers millions of merchants in more than 175 countries whose traffic can spike within seconds — particularly during Black Friday and Cyber Monday — and traditional static environments would have required costly overprovisioning to survive peak demand without compromising reliability for 81M buyers.
solution_summary: Beginning in 2016 Shopify migrated off its own data centers onto Google Cloud and progressively replatformed onto cloud-native, workload-optimized services: Compute Engine N2/N4 for general purpose, C4 for Shop App catalogue search, Z-series machines backing a distributed global database layer and Shop App package tracking, GKE for elastically scaled microservices, Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme for high-performance databases, and accelerator-optimized VMs that took AI workloads (Shopify Magic, Sidekick AI assistant) from PoC to production.
business_outcome: Shopify absorbed record peak commerce traffic without overprovisioning, giving merchants confidence that flash sales, viral campaigns and global expansion are no longer bounded by platform reliability.
metrics: $14.6B in global Black Friday-Cyber Monday weekend sales (27% YoY)
$5.1M per minute peak sales
489M requests per minute at peak traffic
2.2 trillion edge requests over the weekend
14.8 trillion database queries
1.75 trillion database writes
136M packages tracked globally via the Shop App
architecture_clues: Compute Engine N2 and N4 for general-purpose core commerce, C4 for Shop App catalogue search
Distributed global database layer on Compute Engine Z-series machines with high-performance local storage
Microservices on GKE for instant elastic scaling during traffic spikes
Custom machine types tuning CPU-to-memory ratio per workload
Persistent Disk for general storage; Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme for high-performance databases
Accelerator-optimized VMs powering Shopify Magic and Sidekick AI assistant
Classic compute workloads on GKE call AI services through internal APIs; Hyperdisk provides high-throughput delivery
evidence_quotes: Processed $14.6B in global sales, a 27% increase YoY, during Black Friday weekend
489M requests per minute at peak traffic
We chose Google because we needed a partner who could understand and support hyper-scale reliability engineering.
Shopify has also built a distributed global-scale database layer on Compute Engine's Z-series machines, which provide high-performance local storage.
We used Google's accelerator-optimized VMs to take these workloads from PoC to production.

business_problem: Vodafone CZ's commercial teams had to make trading and campaign decisions on day-old data because the on-premises analytics estate was fragmented across a legacy data warehouse, a separate network platform and a separate campaign tool — each with its own integrations — and was operated by external vendors, producing inconsistent figures, costly nine-month upgrade cycles and reports that did not land until afternoon.
solution_summary: Vodafone CZ migrated its entire data warehouse to BigQuery as part of Vodafone Group's wider Google Cloud strategy, consolidating CRM, billing and network traffic into a single platform, and brought integration in-house using CDAP Cloud Data Fusion so its own teams own the pipelines and can iterate without depending on outside suppliers.
business_outcome: Vodafone CZ now operates from a single source of truth with daily data ready before business hours, has eliminated multi-month upgrade outages, is materially cheaper to run on a serverless pay-as-you-go model, and is positioned to migrate ML workloads next.
metrics: 46% reduction in operational costs after migrating to BigQuery
Data delivery moved from day-old to same-day, ready by 9 AM
Eliminated nine-month hardware and software upgrade cycles
architecture_clues: BigQuery as the single warehouse holding CRM, billing and network traffic data
Serverless, pay-as-you-go BigQuery model replacing fixed-capacity on-premises infrastructure
CDAP Cloud Data Fusion (open source) for in-house data integration pipelines, deployed with help from Vodafone's central AI and Data team
Automatic multi-region backup of data, replacing single-location on-premises servers
Per-query cost visibility used as a forcing function to write more efficient pipelines
ML workloads being prepared for migration onto the same BigQuery foundation
evidence_quotes: 46% reduction in operational costs by migrating to BigQuery
Data delivery improved from day-old to same-day by 9AM
First thing each day, all our data is pre-calculated and ready for users
We're now self-sufficient. We integrated all operations inside our teams with Google Cloud.
Every query we run has a cost associated with it, so we're challenged to be better data engineers

business_problem: Accenture's enterprise clients wanted to move past generative AI proofs of concept to production-grade, scalable, compliant applications, but ad hoc development stitched together a dozen tools and could not answer how AI would scale reliably, comply with regulations, and be explainable across regulated industries like energy, healthcare, and financial services.
solution_summary: Accenture built a centralized, responsible generative AI platform on Azure AI Foundry for orchestrating agent-based workflows, evaluating outputs, and maintaining observability across the AI application lifecycle. It uses Azure AI Search for RAG, Azure AI Content Safety for cascading pre- and post-response filters, Azure Functions and Azure App Service for stateful agent workflows, Azure Machine Learning and Azure AI Foundry Models for custom training and fine-tuning, Azure AI Foundry Observability with Azure Monitor and Application Insights for tracing, and is piloting the Microsoft AI Red Teaming Agent for adversarial-prompt simulation.
business_outcome: Accenture reduced the time to build AI applications by up to 50%, projects up to a 30% efficiency increase and 20% cost reduction, has deployed 75+ generative AI use cases with 16+ in production across industries, and delivered 17 use cases in four months for a client that had originally requested 14 over eight months.
metrics: 50% reduction in time to build AI apps
30% increase in overall efficiency (potential)
20% reduction in costs (potential)
75+ generative AI use cases deployed across clients
16+ solutions in full production
17 use cases delivered in 4 months vs 14 originally requested over 8 months
800,000+ Accenture employees
architecture_clues: Azure AI Foundry as unified SDK for safety, accuracy, and performance tracking
Azure AI Search for RAG with indexed, vectorized enterprise knowledge
Azure Functions persist application state, session metadata, and agent memory
Azure App Service for modular, event-driven agent workflows
Two-layer cascading safety nets pre- and post-response using Azure AI Content Safety
Iterative AI red teaming simulations and A/B comparisons against golden datasets
Azure AI Foundry Observability with Azure Monitor + Application Insights for full traceability
Microsoft AI Red Teaming Agent simulates adversarial prompts and detects model and application risk posture
evidence_quotes: Accenture has already deployed 75+ use cases across industries, with 16 in production, reducing AI app build time by 50%.
Azure AI Foundry gives us a unified view across all our applications. Instead of stitching together a dozen tools, we had a single SDK to track safety, accuracy, and performance.
We were able to establish two layers of filtration. Even if one misses something, the second catches it. That’s peace of mind at scale.
Accenture delivered 17 use cases in just four months. That’s the power of having a unified, Azure-native foundation.

business_problem: AT&T's employees were spending too much time investigating across many search links to find answers, developers were debugging code line by line, and data flowed faster than humans could keep up. Leadership needed proof that AI could handle sensitive customer data at enterprise scale, meet strict compliance and security requirements, and deliver measurable ROI across millions of daily customer interactions.
solution_summary: AT&T built Ask AT&T, a generative AI platform on Microsoft Azure, evolving into a multi-agent system orchestrated with Microsoft Foundry, Azure OpenAI, and Microsoft Agent Framework. Specialized agents collaborate—one retrieves verified content via Azure AI Search, another summarizes customer context, another updates call notes. The platform runs on Azure Kubernetes Service with Azure API Management for governance, Azure Cosmos DB and Azure DocumentDB for state and history, Azure Cache for Redis for real-time responsiveness, Azure Storage for governance and analytics, and Azure AI Content Safety, Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and OpenTelemetry-compliant Arize AI for safety and observability.
business_outcome: AT&T deployed 71 governed generative AI solutions to over 100,000 employees, reduced customer-care information-search time by 33%, processes about 9 billion tokens daily, and delivers more than double year-over-year ROI with over two times the free cash flow benefits saved annually through efficiency gains and automation.
metrics: 71 generative AI solutions deployed
100,000+ employees actively using agents
~9 billion tokens processed daily
33% faster information searching for customer care agents
More than 2× free cash flow benefits saved annually
More than double year-over-year ROI
architecture_clues: Multi-agent system orchestrated with Microsoft Foundry and Azure OpenAI
Azure Kubernetes Service for container orchestration of agents
Azure API Management for governance and monitoring of containerized agents
Azure Cosmos DB chosen for high-performance chat/agent apps
Azure DocumentDB stores long-term chat history and master configurations
Azure AI Content Safety filters, data governance policies, and model protection controls
Azure Monitor + Log Analytics + OpenTelemetry-compliant Arize AI for tracing and observability
Legal, security, and finance review before launching every agent
evidence_quotes: With Microsoft, we’ve deployed 71 unique generative AI solutions serving over 100,000 employees—every one of them governed, trusted, and delivering ROI.
We chose Azure Cosmos DB because it is optimized for high-performance chat and agent apps, super-responsive user experiences, and scalability.
Digital coworkers surface answers faster than the old systems, reducing the time spent searching for information during calls by 33% and cutting average handle time.
Approximately 9 billion tokens ... processed daily ... Over two times the free cash flow benefits saved annually through efficiency gains and automation.

business_problem: Bayer's agronomic advisors and frontline teams were spending hours or days navigating crop-protection labels that can exceed 100 pages, escalating to a small tech team and risking delays and errors when giving precise, label-accurate guidance across products, crops, regions, and regulations.
solution_summary: Bayer worked with Microsoft Cloud for Industry to build E.L.Y. Crop Protection (Mini), a domain-specific small language model based on the Microsoft Phi architecture and fine-tuned on Bayer's proprietary product-label data, regulatory rules, and expert-authored Q&A. The model is hosted, versioned, and published in Azure AI Foundry Models, surfaced to internal advisors through Bayer's E.L.Y. Copilot, and extended to strategic retail partners via highly secure APIs with full IP control, audit logging, and source-linked responses.
business_outcome: Bayer is seeing 5–10% productivity gains among early users, resolving complex crop-protection questions in under 30 seconds instead of days or weeks, and is planning to expand the model to more retail partners while extending coverage to seeds, biologicals, carbon products, and additional languages.
metrics: 5–10% productivity gains among early users
Complex questions resolved in under 30 seconds vs days or weeks
Crop-protection labels can exceed 100 pages
architecture_clues: Small language model on Microsoft Phi architecture
Fine-tuned on proprietary Bayer label data and regulatory rules
Hosted in Azure AI Foundry Models with versioning and IP control
Source-linked, audit-logged responses for compliance
Surfaced via existing E.L.Y. Copilot interface and exposed to partners through secure APIs
Aligned to Microsoft's solution accelerator for regulated AI deployments
evidence_quotes: We estimate 5-10% productivity gains among early users. Complex questions that once took days or weeks to resolve now take under 30 seconds.

We fine-tuned Microsoft Phi using our internal datasets. We worked closely with Microsoft’s Cloud for Industry team to shape the model’s architecture and ensure it aligned with our compliance and operational needs.
Azure AI Foundry also gives us IP ownership and lets us control updates, access, and traceability.
The model is hosted in Azure AI Foundry Models, allowing Bayer to retain full IP control while enabling rapid iteration, versioning, and traceability.

business_problem: Ford's nearly 170,000 employees worked across multiple fragmented collaboration platforms—developers in one tool, business teams in another, meetings in a third—creating communication silos, slowing productivity, and complicating support, training, and supplier collaboration across hundreds of global locations.
solution_summary: Ford migrated all employees to Microsoft Teams with Teams Premium, supported by Power Apps, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Microsoft 365. The IT organization built custom connectors so employees could migrate 30 days, 90 days, or a full year of channels, meetings, and chat histories into Teams. Teams Premium provides AI-powered meeting notes, intelligent recap, watermarking, and end-to-end encryption. Ford is moving existing conferencing hardware to Microsoft Teams Rooms and using Teams federation to coordinate with external partners in shared channels.
business_outcome: Ford unified collaboration onto a single secure platform, doubled employee satisfaction scores for collaboration, saved millions by consolidating tools, reduced help-desk tickets and onboarding complexity, accelerated cross-functional and external-partner collaboration, and built a foundation for embedding agentic AI into workflows.
metrics: Nearly 170,000 employees unified on Microsoft Teams
Doubled employee satisfaction scores for collaboration
Saving millions by using a single collaboration tool
architecture_clues: Custom Teams connectors for migrating 30/90/365 days of historical channels, meetings, and chat
Power Apps hosted inside Teams; SharePoint and OneDrive integration
Teams Premium AI-powered meeting notes and intelligent recap
Watermarking and end-to-end encryption for sensitive meetings
Teams federation with external partners via shared channels
Existing conferencing hardware re-used with Microsoft Teams Rooms software
evidence_quotes: Teams became the place where work happens. We’ve doubled our satisfaction scores for collaboration and are saving millions by using a single tool.
Our number one challenge was inconsistencies with how a device is delivered.
Users could choose how much content to bring over: 30 days, 90 days, or a full year.
With Teams federation, Ford is engaging external partners in shared channels, reducing email volume and improving responsiveness.

business_problem: Hertz needed to modernize hundreds of legacy processes—employee shift planning, roadside-assistance lookups across vehicle manuals on SharePoint, and payroll updates—to improve responsiveness for 26,000+ employees serving customers across 11,000+ rental locations in 160 countries.
solution_summary: As part of a technology modernization strategy, Hertz developed low-code apps and agents on Microsoft Power Platform. A 'Start My Day' (Plan My Day) Power App integrates Microsoft Shifts data with Palantir and other work data via Dataverse and Power Automate cloud flows, automating updates into the ADP payroll system. A roadside-assistance agent ('Manny') built on Microsoft Copilot Studio uses generative AI to query vehicle manuals and trusted sites in natural language and is being piloted at one location with plans to scale.
business_outcome: Hertz reduced customer-issue resolution time by over 15% in a pilot of its Copilot Studio agent, streamlined frontline shift planning and payroll integration, and enabled an employee with no prior agent experience to build a production-quality roadside-assistance agent.
metrics: 11,000+ rental locations in 160 countries
26,000+ employees
Fleet of 560,000+ vehicles
Over 15% reduction in time to resolve customer issues during agent pilot
architecture_clues: Plan My Day Power App integrates Microsoft Shifts and Palantir data via Dataverse using Power Automate cloud flows and APIs
Automatic payroll updates pushed to ADP system
Copilot Studio agent ('Manny') uses natural-language queries grounded in a limited set of trusted vehicle-manufacturer sites and a government VIN lookup
Agent extended with links to instructional video platforms
Future plans: image recognition in Copilot Studio and multi-agent orchestration
evidence_quotes: An early pilot of its agent, built on Copilot Studio has already reduced the time to resolve customer issues by over 15%.
We have literally hundreds of processes that could benefit from automation and AI, and we see Power Platform as a fast, efficient way to develop solutions that deliver those benefits.
Work shift data from Microsoft Shifts and Palantir moved to Dataverse using Power Automate cloud flows and APIs.
This is actually the first agent that I’ve created. Even if I could do this with custom code, it would have taken a lot longer.

business_problem: Levi Strauss & Co.'s push to become a fan-obsessed, direct-to-consumer brand created an influx of data that overwhelmed inconsistent end-user devices—employees experienced crashes when handling 20,000-line reporting files—and varied processes for joining meetings and accessing applications slowed productivity across the workforce.
solution_summary: Levi Strauss & Co. standardized on Microsoft Intune across all endpoints, accelerated to Windows 11, and rolled out Surface Copilot+ PCs with Microsoft 365 Copilot. Employees and central teams have built Copilot agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot—an 'Ask Ben' benefits agent and a Standard Operating Procedure audit agent—and the company is partnering with Microsoft on 'Rivet', a Microsoft Teams-fronted conversational super-agent that orchestrates multiple behind-the-scenes agents.
business_outcome: Levi Strauss & Co. delivers a consistent, AI-enabled employee experience across the workforce, eliminated reporting crashes for finance teams, and uses Copilot agents to compress year-long projects to a single day while moving from agent concept to executive demo in three weeks.
metrics: Project review time reduced from up to one year to one day
Agent concept to executive demo in three weeks
Monthly reporting task involves about 20,000 lines of data
architecture_clues: Microsoft Intune as universal endpoint management foundation
Windows 11 standardized across the company
Surface Copilot+ PCs with dedicated Copilot key
Microsoft 365 Copilot embedded in everyday tools and Windows
Custom Copilot agents ('Ask Ben') for HR/benefits navigation
SOP audit agent built on Copilot
Rivet: Teams-fronted conversational super-agent orchestrating multiple Copilot agents
evidence_quotes: With Copilot, we had results in a day. ... Stirling estimates that reviewing them would take up to a year, and 'a painful one,' at that.
With Copilot on Windows, building an agent was so simple that my team went from concept to presenting a demo to the executive team in three weeks.
One of the first steps in our journey was to deploy Microsoft Intune across all endpoints in the company.
A Copilot+ PC running on Windows 11, it really just makes folks’ lives easy and helps us provide an amazing employee experience.

business_problem: St. Luke’s—with 15 campuses, 300 outpatient sites, 23,000+ employees, and 2.5+ petabytes of patient data—needed unified, real-time visibility across a previously disconnected security stack so its SOC could anticipate and disrupt phishing and DDoS attacks before they paralyzed life-saving care delivery.
solution_summary: St. Luke’s deployed Microsoft Security Copilot as the AI layer across Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Entra, Microsoft Purview, and Microsoft Intune. The SOC is an early adopter of multiple Security Copilot agents—the Security Alert Triage Agent (formerly Phishing Triage Agent) in Defender, the Conditional Access Optimization Agent in Entra, the Vulnerability Remediation Agent in Intune, and Alert Triage Agents in Purview DLP and IRM—to autonomously triage thousands of phishing alerts, generate incident reports, and correlate insights across endpoints, email, identity, applications, and cloud workloads.
business_outcome: St. Luke’s saves nearly 200 hours monthly with the Security Alert Triage Agent autonomously closing thousands of false-positive phishing alerts, reduced incident-report creation from hours to minutes, shifted analysts from reactive triage to proactive threat hunting, and uses Security Copilot’s gap analysis to drive its security roadmap.
metrics: Nearly 200 hours saved monthly with Security Alert Triage Agent
Incident reports created in minutes instead of hours
15 campuses and 300 outpatient sites
2.5+ petabytes of patient data in motion
23,000+ employees
architecture_clues: Security Copilot as connective tissue across Defender, Sentinel, Entra, Purview, and Intune
Security Alert Triage Agent uses advanced language-model analysis to classify reported phishing emails
Conditional Access Optimization Agent in Entra
Vulnerability Remediation Agent in Intune
Alert Triage Agents in Purview DLP and IRM
Agent explanations in plain text used by analysts to validate decisions
evidence_quotes: The Security Alert Triage Agent is a game changer. It’s saving us nearly 200 hours monthly by autonomously handling and closing thousands of false positive alerts.
Creating an incident report by hand can take hours, but with Copilot, the process is reduced to minutes.
Healthcare is the number one cyberattack target in the world.
Our team shifted from reactive triage to proactive threat hunting, now that they’re not bogged down by routine triage.
